description: A large Arctic bear that depends on sea ice to hunt seals and travel between feeding grounds.
A small fox adapted to extreme cold, scavenging and hunting rodents and seabirds across tundra landscapes.
A tusked marine mammal that forages on the seafloor for clams and other invertebrates near sea-ice edges.
The largest penguin, breeding on sea ice and diving deep for fish and squid in frigid Southern Ocean waters.
A seal that maintains breathing holes in sea ice and feeds on fish and cephalopods under the ice.
A high-altitude cat with thick fur and long tail, hunting ibex and blue sheep across rugged alpine terrain.
A small arboreal mammal in mountain forests that feeds primarily on bamboo and seasonal fruits.
A keystone megaherbivore that shapes savanna ecosystems by browsing trees, grazing grasses, and digging for water.
A social big cat living in prides, preying on antelope and other ungulates in open grasslands and savannas.
The fastest land animal, specialized for high-speed chases of small to medium antelope in open habitats.
A tall browsing herbivore that feeds on acacia leaves and helps shape woodland structure through selective browsing.
A semi-aquatic grazer that spends days in water and forages on grasses at night, influencing nutrient flows.
A large crocodile and apex predator of African rivers and lakes, ambushing fish, birds, and mammals.
A great ape living in high-elevation forests, feeding on leaves, shoots, and stems in cohesive family groups.
A forest-dwelling relative of the giraffe, browsing understory plants in dense rainforests of the Congo Basin.
A highly intelligent primate using tools and complex social behavior, eating fruits, insects, and occasional meat.
A marine turtle grazing seagrass meadows and algae, connecting reef and coastal ecosystems through migration.
A reef-associated turtle with a narrow beak, feeding largely on sponges and helping maintain reef diversity.
A powerful big cat of the Americas, hunting across forests and wetlands and preying on capybaras and caimans.
A large forest raptor that hunts arboreal mammals like sloths and monkeys in tall tropical rainforest canopies.
A slow-moving arboreal mammal with low metabolism, feeding mainly on leaves and spending most time in trees.
A small amphibian with toxic skin alkaloids derived from diet, feeding on ants and other small invertebrates.
A large constrictor snake living in slow-moving waters, ambushing fish, birds, and mammals in wetlands.
The world's largest rodent, living in social groups near water and grazing aquatic plants and grasses.
A social mustelid of tropical rivers, hunting fish cooperatively and relying on clean waterways and riparian cover.
A large grazer that shapes prairie vegetation through grazing and wallowing, supporting grassland biodiversity.
A pack-hunting canid that preys on deer and elk, affecting trophic cascades in forest and mountain ecosystems.
A large raptor often near water, feeding on fish and waterfowl and using tall trees or cliffs for nesting.
A large deer adapted to cold regions, browsing shrubs and aquatic plants across boreal forests and marshes.
A widespread bear that eats berries, roots, insects, and fish, including salmon runs in northern rivers.
A nocturnal burrowing mammal that feeds on earthworms, insects, and fruits across mixed farmland and woods.
A large deer of forests and moorlands, grazing and browsing and serving as prey for large carnivores where present.
A highly specialized predator that feeds mainly on rabbits in Mediterranean shrublands and open woodlands.
A migratory deer adapted to cold climates, feeding on lichens and shrubs and traveling long distances seasonally.
An arboreal marsupial specializing on eucalyptus leaves, with low-energy lifestyle and strong habitat dependence.
A large hopping marsupial grazing grasses and forbs in open habitats, adapted to dry conditions and variable rainfall.
A semi-aquatic monotreme that forages underwater for insect larvae and small invertebrates in streams and rivers.
A small reef fish living among sea anemones, feeding on plankton and algae and benefiting from symbiosis.
A large monitor lizard that scavenges and hunts deer and pigs, using ambush tactics in warm, seasonal habitats.
A great ape living mostly in the forest canopy, feeding on fruit, leaves, and insects and sensitive to deforestation.
A critically endangered tiger adapted to dense forests, hunting deer and wild pigs in island rainforests.
A large one-horned rhinoceros inhabiting floodplain grasslands and wetlands, grazing and browsing in tall grasses.
A big cat found in diverse habitats including mangroves and forests, preying on deer and wild boar.
A bear with specialized diet of bamboo, living in mountainous forests and relying on intact bamboo understory.
A migratory crane breeding in Siberian wetlands, feeding on aquatic plants and invertebrates along flyways.
A baleen whale known for long migrations and complex songs, feeding on krill and small fish via bubble-net feeding.
The largest animal on Earth, feeding primarily on krill in productive cold-water upwelling zones.
A marine mammal grazing seagrass in shallow coastal waters, vulnerable to habitat loss and boat strikes.
A small desert fox with large ears for heat dissipation, feeding on insects, small vertebrates, and fruits.
A desert-adapted ungulate capable of long periods without water, browsing tough vegetation in arid landscapes.
A long-distance migratory seabird that breeds in Arctic regions and feeds on small fish and invertebrates.
A unique lizard that forages on marine algae and basks on volcanic rocks, adapted to saltwater environments.
A large predatory shark of temperate coastal waters, feeding on fish and marine mammals and patrolling seal colonies.
A large scavenging bird relying on carrion and open landscapes, historically threatened by lead poisoning.
A migratory butterfly whose larvae feed on milkweed, undertaking multi-generational migrations across North America.
A social lemur of Madagascar feeding on fruits, leaves, and insects, using scent communication and sunbathing.
A nocturnal lemur using an elongated finger to tap wood and extract insect larvae, adapted to forest foraging.
A large frugivorous bird that disperses seeds in rainforests, also eating small animals and nesting in tree cavities.
A freshwater dolphin navigating complex river channels and flooded forests, feeding on diverse fish species.
A migratory antelope of the Eurasian steppe with a distinctive nose that filters dust and warms cold air.
